original_hypothesis.title: AgentToken.setProjectTaxRates can set basis points > 10000 leading to unchecked underflow and massive balance inflation (token economics break)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AgentToken’s setProjectTaxRates() does not enforce tax bounds (basis points < 10000) while _taxProcessing performs amountLessTax_ -= tax inside an unchecked block. If tax > sentAmount, subtraction underflows, producing a near-uint256.max transfer amount credited to the recipient, effectively minting an enormous balance and breaking supply invariants.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete arithmetic defect with a clearly described invariant violation and catastrophic impact if triggered. Realism is LOW because it requires privileged caller (owner/factory), but it is still a code defect worth validating and remediating because it can brick markets or be triggered accidentally during admin operations.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setProjectTaxRates assigns arbitrary uint16; _taxProcessing uses unchecked and subtracts tax from sentAmount without enforcing tax<=sentAmount

preconditions: Privileged caller (owner or factory) can call setProjectTaxRates
Taxed transfer path is hit (buy/sell where applyTax=true and !_autoSwapInProgress)
Configured liquidity pool recognized by isLiquidityPool(from/to)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: unchecked subtraction with user-controlled (admin-set) tax rates
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: If basis points > 10000, computed tax can exceed sentAmount causing underflow
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No require(newTax < BP_DENOM); no cap; no monotonic decrease enforcement

analysis_questions: Is there any other validation layer (factory or UI) preventing tax rates > 10000?
Do any code paths set taxes automatically from encoded params that could be malformed?
What happens to _balances and _totalSupply accounting after underflow-minted transfer?
validation_requirements: Unit test: setProjectTaxRates(10001,10001) then execute sell/buy transfer and observe recipient balance inflation
Check whether _balances[address(this)] is also increased by tax, compounding distortion
Check if later transfers/autoSwap become impossible due to huge balances

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Privileged operator sets sell tax to 20000 bp, then any sell to LP underflows amountLessTax and credits astronomical tokens to LP (or to buyer on buy), destroying price and breaking integrations; attacker could also route to self to gain enormous balance.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Catastrophic market break; potential total loss for holders due to price collapse / broken supply
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All AgentToken holders and LP participants
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Very limited without token migration or admin intervention

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing fuzz/property tests enforcing tax bounds and no-underflow invariant

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor tax rate updates > 10000; sudden massive Transfer amounts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
